Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Building a Procedural Hex Map with Wave Function Collapse 511 upvotes, 72 comments
  2. Optimizing Top K in Postgres 89 upvotes, 12 comments
  3. Show HN: Remotely use my guitar tuner 211 upvotes, 48 comments
  4. An opinionated take on how to do important research that matters 136 upvotes, 33 comments
  5. Darkrealms BBS 107 upvotes, 25 comments
  6. TCXO Failure Analysis 16 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. I put my whole life into a single database 25 upvotes, 3 comments
  8. JSLinux Now Supports x86_64 333 upvotes, 101 comments
  9. Two Years of Emacs Solo 271 upvotes, 81 comments
  10. Getting Started in Common Lisp 50 upvotes, 10 comments
  11. Graphing how the 10k* most common English words define each other 73 upvotes, 20 comments
  12. The Gervais Principle, or the Office According to "The Office" 40 upvotes, 9 comments
  13. Lotus 1-2-3 on the PC with DOS 106 upvotes, 38 comments
  14. Notes on Baking at the South Pole 65 upvotes, 23 comments
  15. LoGeR – 3D reconstruction from extremely long videos (DeepMind, UC Berkeley) 56 upvotes, 19 comments
  16. The hidden compile-time cost of C++26 reflection 31 upvotes, 10 comments
  17. OpenAI is walking away from expanding its Stargate data center with Oracle 372 upvotes, 220 comments
  18. The “JVG algorithm” only wins on tiny numbers 71 upvotes, 38 comments
  19. No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user 246 upvotes, 175 comments
  20. macOS Tahoe windows have different corner radiuses 180 upvotes, 127 comments
  21. Launch HN: Terminal Use (YC W26) – Vercel for filesystem-based agents 105 upvotes, 73 comments
  22. Show HN: DenchClaw – Local CRM on Top of OpenClaw 123 upvotes, 97 comments
  23. Redox OS has adopted a Certificate of Origin policy and a strict no-LLM policy 158 upvotes, 129 comments
  24. Learnings from paying artists royalties for AI-generated art 138 upvotes, 114 comments
  25. DARPA’s new X-76 205 upvotes, 183 comments
  26. Yann LeCun's AI startup raises $1B in Europe's largest ever seed round 50 upvotes, 36 comments
  27. Is legal the same as legitimate: AI reimplementation and the erosion of copyleft 483 upvotes, 500 comments
  28. Show HN: I Was Here – Draw on street view, others can find your drawings 35 upvotes, 25 comments
  29. No leap second will be introduced at the end of June 2026 121 upvotes, 128 comments
  30. Florida judge rules red light camera tickets are unconstitutional 438 upvotes, 556 comments

Last generated 7:30pm, 10 March 2026. Source here.
